title: T7 Self-learning: KDD structural SDLC gates
prompt: Build David's stated real direction for KDD-reading (PO feedback 2026-07-04, recorded at the bottom of backlog/2026-07-04-kdd-read-gate-handover.done.md and in ADR-0028's Revision Log): structural SDLC gates, not session-start advisories — Gate A 'before developing, validate against KDD decisions' and Gate B 'absorbing worker output → write back through Lisa'. The kdd-bridge MCP was the mechanism; it serves these checkpoints, it does not replace them. In dark-factory (no PRs; David works on main) Gate A attaches to ticket dispatch: add a single-line KDD-decisions checkpoint to the ungated branch of engine/orchestrator.py task_prompt() (check accepted docs/kdd/decisions before first edit; on contradiction park engine/store/needs-decision/<tid> and reply BLOCKED_BY_DECISION, no edits). Gate B is ticket-shaped because the engine can never run Lisa headlessly: write stdlib-only engine/kdd_absorb.py with --status (diff store/done/ against the append-only ledger engine/store/kdd-absorbed.jsonl; ledger is written later by the absorption worker, never by this ticket) and --emit-ticket (emit ONE batched, status:'deferred' Lisa-absorption ticket into store/queue/ — dispatchable() skips deferred tickets — with a self-exclusion guard so absorption tickets are never their own input and never two in flight). Also write the design doc docs/kdd/sdlc-gates.md (both gate contracts, the advisory-first enforcement ladder per ADR-0028's own preference, honest v1 limitation that an ungated park reads as failed(timeout) since HITL polling is gated-only, triggers deliberately unwired — no cron, cross-repo PR-form as future scope only), plus three append-only wiring edits (docs/kdd/index.md pointer, one ADR-0028 Revision Log line, one engine/README.md layout line), a proof report at backlog/wargames/proof/T7-09/REPORT.md, and a commit+push. Advisory-first everywhere; do NOT touch the reap loop, dispatchable(), resume_prompt(), status.py, .claude/settings.json, .mcp.json, appydave-plugins, or kdd-bridge; do NOT run orchestrator.py main (you are likely being run by it); do NOT hand-write any KDD learnings/decisions yourself — absorption goes through Lisa in a later worker session David un-defers. Read and execute the war game at backlog/wargames/T7-09-kdd-sdlc-gates.md. Follow its moves in order; on any failure signal apply the counter-move; on any abort condition stop and write needs-decision/.
verify: docs/kdd/sdlc-gates.md exists (<=200 lines, names Gate A, Gate B, and 'advisory'). engine/orchestrator.py contains 'GATE-A' and both files pass python3 -m py_compile; from engine/, python3 -c importing orchestrator shows task_prompt('x.json') contains GATE-A with no newline in the returned string while task_prompt('x.json', gated=True) does NOT contain GATE-A. python3 engine/kdd_absorb.py --status exits 0; exactly one engine/store/queue/kdd-absorb-*.json exists (or store/kdd-absorb-DRAFT.json if the war game's F2 Route B fired) with status 'deferred', executor 'swagger', and 'lisa' in its prompt; a second --emit-ticket exits 1 (guard). tail of docs/kdd/index.md mentions sdlc-gates; docs/kdd/decisions/0028-*.md gained a wg-t7-09 Revision Log line with 0 deleted lines in the diff; engine/README.md mentions kdd_absorb. backlog/wargames/proof/T7-09/REPORT.md exists; a commit mentioning wg-t7-09 or SDLC gates exists. Negative: engine/store/kdd-absorbed.jsonl does NOT exist yet, no kdd-absorb ticket in store/done/ (the absorption run did not execute), the commit touches neither .claude/settings.json nor .mcp.json nor docs/kdd/learnings|patterns/, git -C ~/dev/ad/appydave-plugins diff --quiet passes, and the orchestrator diff touches no function other than task_prompt.
